cards.text: To iterate through all elements of a vector with indices, use a {{c1::for loop}} with index from {{c2::0}} to {{c3::std::ssize(vec) - 1}} or {{c3::vec.size() - 1}}.

cards.question: Write a loop to print all elements of a vector using indices
cards.explanation: Use signed int for index, std::ssize() for signed length, iterate from 0 to size-1.

cards.text: Three main ways to iterate a vector: {{c1::index-based}} for loop, {{c2::iterator-based}} for loop, and {{c3::range-based}} for loop.
The most common way to iterate through a vector when you need the index is an {{c1::index-based for loop}}. The most common way when you only need values is a {{c2::range-based for loop}}.

cards.question: What does this code output?
cards.explanation: The loop doubles each element. v[2] was 3, becomes 6.

cards.concept: When to use index-based vs range-based for loops
cards.explanation:
Use index-based when:
  • You need the index position
  • You need to access multiple elements at once (e.g., i and i+1)
  • You need to iterate in reverse or skip elements
Use range-based when:
  • You only need to access elements sequentially
  • Simpler and more readable
  • Less error-prone
examples: for (int i{0}; i < std::ssize(v); ++i)  // need index
for (const auto& val : v)  // just values

cards.question: What's the problem with this loop?
cards.answer: Off-by-one error and signed/unsigned comparison. Should be i < std::ssize(v), not <=
cards.explanation: Using <= goes one past the end (undefined behavior). Also comparing signed int with unsigned size().

cards.text: To iterate backwards through a vector with indices: {{c1::for (int i{std::ssize(vec) - 1}; i >= 0; --i)}}
You can't easily iterate backwards using std::size_t because it's {{c1::unsigned}}. When i is 0 and decremented, it {{c2::wraps}} to the {{c3::maximum unsigned value}} instead of -1.

cards.question: Write a loop to iterate backwards and print vector elements
cards.explanation: Start at size-1, use signed int, decrement until i < 0.

cards.text: Accessing a vector index beyond its size causes {{c1::undefined behavior}}. Always ensure index is within valid range {{c2::[0, size-1]}}.
When iterating through a vector, prefer {{c1::range-based for loops}} for simplicity when you don't need indices, and {{c2::index-based loops}} when you need positional information.
